{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the steepest descent method with exact line searches applied to the convex quadratic function\n\\[\nf(x)=\\frac{1}{2}x^TQx-b^Tx,\n\\]\nwhere \\(Q\\) is symmetric and positive definite. Using the properties developed in this chapter, show that if the initial point satisfies \\(x_0-x^*\\) parallel to an eigenvector of \\(Q\\), then the steepest descent method finds the solution in one step."}
